resources.string: Compare benchmark runs by tapping the **Baseline** chip on any run result card to set the run as the comparison baseline. We\'ll automatically calculate and display the percentage differences in **all other** run result cards.
Ensure that the sum of prefill and decode tokens remains under the model\'s maximum token limit (%d vs %d)
Are you sure you want to delete the benchmark result?
Are you sure you want to delete the downloaded model \"%1$s\"?
Learn more and see model license
The model you\'ve selected may exceed your device\'s memory, which can cause the app to crash. For the best experience, we recommend trying a smaller model.
Setting max tokens above 10000 may cause app to be unstable.
This process typically takes several minutes and cannot be interrupted once started. Would you like to proceed?
Running a benchmark while the server has a model loaded may cause out-of-memory errors or GPU resource conflicts. Please stop the server first, then try again.
Tap on graph to see value
Manage the sources your models are downloaded from.

Model sources provide lists of models available for download. OlliteRT ships with a built-in source that is kept up to date automatically.

You can add custom sources by providing a URL to a compatible model list. Disabling a source hides its models from the download list but does not remove any models already downloaded.

Models that appear in multiple sources are shown once, using the first enabled source.
No compatible models found. Update to version %1$s or newer.
No models found in your model sources. Try refreshing or adding a new source.
Models were found but none could be loaded. The model source may contain invalid data.
%1$s hidden — requires a newer app version
The server needs Wi-Fi to accept connections from other devices. You can still start the server, but it will only be reachable via localhost.
This will permanently delete all logs — both the current session and the database. This cannot be undone.
You currently have %1$d logs. Reducing the limit to %2$d will remove the %3$d oldest entries after saving.
You have unsaved changes. Would you like to save or discard them?
Some of the changed settings require a server restart to take effect.

This will hard-cut prompts that exceed the context window, discarding content from the beginning — including system prompts, tool definitions, and earlier conversation history.

The model will receive incomplete context, which typically results in incoherent, off-topic, or nonsensical responses.

Only enable this if requests are being rejected for exceeding context limits and no other strategy (truncate history, compact schemas) is sufficient.
This will reset all settings to their default values, including port, bearer token, HuggingFace token, inference configs, system prompts, and all toggles.
This will reset all settings to their default values, including port, bearer token, HuggingFace token, inference configs, system prompts, and all toggles.\n\nThe server will be stopped.
This will reset all inference settings to their default values for this model. The model will reload to apply the changes.
This is a gated model. Please click the button below to view and agree to the user agreement. After accepting, simply close that tab to proceed with the model download.
The model could not be found on HuggingFace. Verify that the model ID and commit hash in your model list are correct.
This model is gated and requires a HuggingFace access token to download.\n\nPlease add your token in Settings.
Your HuggingFace token was rejected by the server. It may be expired, revoked, or incorrect.\n\nPlease check your token in Settings.

This download requires %1$.1f GB total:
 • Model size: %2$.1f GB
 • System reserve: %3$.0f GB

Available: %4$.1f GB

Free up at least %5$.1f GB to continue, or tap "Download Anyway" to skip the safety check.

This import requires %1$.1f GB total:
 • Model size: %2$.1f GB
 • System reserve: %3$.0f GB

Available: %4$.1f GB

Free up at least %5$.1f GB to continue, or tap "Import Anyway" to skip the safety check.
Looks like the model is a web-only model and is not supported by the app.
This will unload \"%1$s\" and load \"%2$s\".\n\nThe server will restart.
Import model list from JSON file
The model list contains no models
This model list requires a newer app version
This URL uses an unencrypted connection. Data may be intercepted on untrusted networks.
Import model list from a direct JSON URL
These are app defaults, not recommended values for this model. Adjust them now or later in the model\'s inference settings for best results.
Select the accelerators supported by this model
A model named \"%1$s\" is already imported. Replacing it will overwrite the existing model file and settings.
A model with this name is already imported
This name is used by a model in the repository
Name can only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, and underscores
Please rename the model file or use a different name to avoid conflicts with repository models.
If you find OlliteRT useful, consider supporting its development.
This app is built and maintained by a solo developer in their spare time. If it\'s been useful to you, consider supporting its development.
Don\'t ask again for this model
Please wait for the current model to finish loading
Please wait, this may take a moment…
Settings saved. Restart the server manually for changes to take effect.
Settings saved — model will reload after loading finishes
Log data too large for clipboard (%1$d entries) — use Export instead.
Configure server behavior, authentication, and preferences
Prevent screen from turning off while app is open
Show full request and response bodies in the Logs tab
Wrap long lines in log cards instead of scrolling horizontally
Show model output as it generates in the Logs tab for streaming requests

Replace inline base64 image data with a size placeholder when storing log entries. Prevents UI lag from rendering multi-MB image payloads. Disable for full raw data in logs (may cause lag with large images)
Show client device names instead of IP addresses in log entries. Uses reverse DNS lookup which may add slight latency to request logging
Suppress /health endpoint entries from the Logs tab. Useful when monitoring tools poll frequently and create noise
Automatically clear in-memory logs when the server stops
Show a confirmation dialog before clearing logs
Preserve incomplete response text in logs when a streaming request is cancelled by the client
Return an error immediately when a request is already being processed, instead of queueing
Required for downloading models from Hugging Face. Get your token from
Default: 8000. Requires server restart to take effect
Protect the API with a bearer token. Clients must include it in the Authorization header
Origins allowed to make cross-origin requests. Use * to allow all, comma-separated URLs for specific origins (e.g. http://localhost:3000, https://my-app.com), or leave blank to disable CORS
Update the foreground notification after each request to show the total request count
No downloaded models. Download a model first from the Models tab
Automatically load this model when the app is launched
Select a default model above to enable
Launch server automatically when device starts
Model stays in RAM by default. Enable to unload after idle timeout and free memory. Next request auto-reloads (cold start)
How long the model stays loaded after the last request. When the timeout expires, native memory is freed. The next request triggers an automatic reload (5–30s cold start depending on model size)
Some manufacturers kill background apps aggressively. Check dontkillmyapp.com for device-specific fixes
Check for a newer version of OlliteRT %1$s. Only checks releases matching your install channel
Check for the latest version of OlliteRT across all channels (stable, beta, dev)
Update check timed out — check your network connection
Periodically check in the background and notify when an update is available
Notification access not enabled. Grant notification permission in system settings to receive update alerts
Update notification channel is muted in system settings
How often to check for new releases. Default: 24 hours. Min: 1 hour, Max: 30 days
Get notified when releases are available on other channels (beta, dev, stable).\nEach channel installs as a separate app — you can run them side by side
Dev builds already receive all release notifications
Customize which notification channels are enabled
Cross-channel notification channels are muted in system settings
Show text, vision, and audio request counts on the Status screen
Display prefill speed, inter-token latency, latency stats, and context utilization on the Status screen
Save activity logs to a local database so they survive app restarts. Disabled by default
Maximum entries kept in memory and in the database. Oldest entries are pruned when the limit is reached.\nSet to 0 for no limit (capped at 10,000 in memory)
Persisted logs older than this are automatically removed from the database. Does not affect the current session\'s in-memory logs.\nSet to 0 to disable
Remove all logs from the database and current session
Enable server control endpoints (/v1/server/*) for remote management via Home Assistant or other automation tools

Instructs the model to transcribe audio instead of replying to it. Enable for voice assistants that expect transcribed text only. Required for multimodal models that otherwise respond conversationally. Note: applies to all clients using the audio endpoint, which may affect non-voice-assistant workflows (e.g. Open WebUI)
The instruction sent to the model when Force Transcription is enabled. The default prompt will be used if left blank
Default prompt will be used if left blank
Generates a configuration.yaml snippet with your current IP (%1$s), port (%2$d)%3$s
Send a test message when the model loads to verify the engine is working. Disabling this speeds up model startup
Load the vision backend when a multimodal model starts, even before any image request arrives. Eliminates delay on the first image request but increases memory and GPU usage from the start
Enable per-model system prompt field in Inference Settings. The system prompt is prepended to every conversation as an instruction to the model
When a request exceeds the model\'s context window, drop older messages from the conversation while keeping system prompts and the most recent messages

⚠️ Last resort — will likely produce degraded output. Hard-cuts the prompt to fit the context window, discarding the beginning (including system prompts and tool definitions). Only enable if requests are consistently rejected for exceeding context limits
Discard temperature, top_p, top_k, and max_tokens values sent by API clients. The server\'s own Inference Settings will always be used instead

Injects tool schemas directly into the model\'s context via the LiteRT SDK instead of embedding them in the text prompt. May produce more reliable tool calls with compatible models (Gemma 4). Also enables word-by-word streaming — without this, responses are buffered and sent all at once when tools are present, even if the client requests streaming. Disable if tool calling stops working
Display badges and pin recommended models to the top of the list
Manage model sources and allowlist URLs
Logs additional details: full stack traces, memory snapshots, model config, per-request timing. May impact performance
Share the system log buffer for this app. Useful for diagnosing issues
Start a model from the Models tab to begin serving requests.
Idle — model unloaded to free RAM
Start a model from the Models tab

Token counts (Tokens In, Tokens Out) and metrics derived from them (Decode Speed, Prefill Speed, Throughput, Inter-Token Latency) are estimates based on character length ÷ 4.\n\nThe LiteRT SDK does not expose exact token counts, so this approximation is used instead. Actual values may vary depending on the tokenizer.
This will restart the server and reload %1$s. Any in-flight requests will be interrupted.
This will delete all %1$d log entries (not just the %2$d shown). This action cannot be undone.
This will delete all %1$d log entries. This action cannot be undone.

plurals.name: logs_dialog_clear_active_body
plurals.item:
This will delete all log entries. There is %1$d request being processed or queued. Do you want to:

• "Clear" — Delete all logs, let generation continue
• "Stop" — Cancel all pending requests and delete all logs

This will delete all log entries. There are %1$d requests being processed or queued. Do you want to:

• "Clear" — Delete all logs, let generation continue
• "Stop" — Cancel all pending requests and delete all logs

plurals.name: logs_dialog_stop_active_body
plurals.item: There is %1$d request currently being processed or waiting in the queue. Stopping the server will cancel it.
There are %1$d requests currently being processed or waiting in the queue. Stopping the server will cancel them.

resources.string: No logs match the current filters.
Android is critically low on RAM — the app may be killed soon. Close other apps or use a smaller model.
Turn your device into a local OpenAI-compatible API server with text, vision, and speech-to-text support
You\u2019ll be asked for notification and battery optimization permissions to keep the server running reliably.
Select your model and tap start
Point any OpenAI-compatible app to your phone’s local network IP address
Missing %1$s — the server may be stopped by the system while running in the background. Tap to open settings.
Sit tight — this may take a couple of minutes depending on your device
Stop the server first to run benchmarks
Stop the server first to update the model
Please wait for the current model to finish loading
Failed to load — check Logs for details
Update check failed — check your network connection
Update check failed — rate limited, try again later
Update check failed — repository or releases not found
Update check failed — server error, try again later
Notifies when corrupted data was detected and reset
The %1$s data file was corrupted and has been reset to defaults.
%1$d data files were corrupted and have been reset to defaults: %2$s
DataStore corruption recovered — reset to defaults: %1$s
Notifies when a new version of OlliteRT is available
Notifications about new beta releases from other channels.
Notifications about new dev pre-releases from other channels.
Notifications when newer model versions are available
A newer version of %1$s is available
A newer version of this model is available.
Stop the server before updating this model.
Enables the model\'s thinking mode for step-by-step reasoning
This model does not support thinking mode
Predicts multiple tokens at once for faster decode speed without quality loss
This model does not support speculative decoding

The OpenCL library required for GPU inference is not accessible on this device. This is a known limitation of the current version of LiteRT LM on some phones (e.g. Pixel 5).

The app will use CPU instead — inference works correctly but is slower. Sampler settings (TopK, TopP, Temperature) may not take effect.

If you'd like GPU support for your device, consider raising an issue at the
Prepended to every conversation as a system instruction
e.g. You are a helpful coding assistant…
Changes to Max Tokens or Accelerator will reload the model
Values must be within the allowed range
Requires model update to take effect
The model engine could not be initialized. This usually means the selected accelerator (GPU/NPU) is not supported on this device.\n\nTry switching to CPU in the benchmark settings.
The device ran out of memory while loading the model. Try closing other apps or using a smaller model.
Server is running this model — changes take effect after it reloads. Saved inference customizations will be reset to these new defaults.
Saved inference customizations will be reset to these new defaults.
Invalid CORS origins — use *, blank, or comma-separated URLs with http(s)://
Not enough storage space to complete the download. Partial files have been cleaned up.
Download timed out. Check your internet connection and try again.
No internet connection. Check your network settings and try again.
Network connection was lost during download. Try again when your connection is stable.
Download failed due to a network error. Check your connection and try again.
Model not found (404). Check the model ID and commit hash in your model list.
Authentication failed. Check your HuggingFace token or verify the model is publicly accessible.
Server returned HTTP %1$d. Try again later.
Download failed: the model archive appears corrupted or contains invalid paths. Try downloading again.
Model \'%1$s\' not found. Check that the model is still downloaded or update the default model in Settings.
Model file not found on disk. The model may have been deleted — re-download or update the default model in Settings.
Not enough storage to load model (%1$sMB available, need at least %2$sMB). Free up space and try again.
Showing cached model list — couldn\'t check for updates
Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are supported
Access denied — private repositories are not supported
No file found at this URL
Could not load a valid model list from this URL
This URL does not contain a valid model list
This repository requires a newer version of OlliteRT
Failed to save repository data to disk
Enter the URL of a model list JSON file
Remove %1$s? This won\'t delete any downloaded models.
You have %1$d downloaded model(s) from this source. They will remain on the Models screen until you delete them.
%1$d model(s) from this source are currently downloading. Disabling will cancel the download(s).
%1$d model(s) from this source are currently downloading. Deleting will cancel the download(s).
Model file appears corrupted or truncated (%1$d bytes): %2$s
Model file is corrupted (size mismatch: %1$s on disk, expected %2$s). Please delete the model and download it again.
Insufficient storage to load model (%1$sMB free, need at least %2$sMB). Free up space by deleting unused models or files, then try again.
Try enabling prompt compaction (Truncate History, Compact Tool Schemas, or Trim Prompt) in OlliteRT Settings, or send shorter prompts.
Try sending a shorter prompt or restarting the server.
Check the Models screen for available downloaded models.
The model files may have been deleted. Try re-downloading the model from the Models screen.
The port is already in use by another app. Close the app occupying the port or change the port number in Settings.
The model is not loaded. Wait for loading to complete or restart the server.
The base64 image data could not be decoded. Verify the data URI is correctly formatted (data:image/…;base64,…) and the base64 payload is not truncated.
The device ran out of memory. Try closing other apps, using a smaller model, or reducing Max Tokens in Inference Settings.
